news.title: Football Daily | A 64-team World Cup? More is more when it comes to Fifa-land
news.lede: After a century of matches, and for the first time in history, the World Cup has entered its sixth week. But is it about to get longer? Gianni Infantino didn’t even get through the first of an agonising two-day rest period before dropping the hint that Fifa would be “examining and discussing” the potential for the 2030 tournament to be a 64-team affair. Some may say – not Football Daily, of course – that this was a handy way to move the news cycle on after Balogun-gate, Cable-gate and the ever-growing industry of suspicion that is VAR-gate.

news.title: Rodri: ‘France are one of the best teams here, in great form, but so are Spain. We can beat them’
news.lede: The Spain captain on his team’s chances in the World Cup semi-final, analysing every game so far and how to get the best out of Lamine Yamal. “I’m not that bad,” Rodri Hernández says. It’s the morning after the World Cup’s 100th game and in a conference room at the Cotton Bowl where old posters line the walls and Spain are about to start their penultimate training session before the 101st match, their captain is doing the calculations. Even with the six in which he’s played and all the travel, from Atlanta to Guadalajara and Dallas to Los Angeles and back, 9,000 miles so far, he reckons he has watched the “immense majority”.

news.title: The most expensive World Cup 2026 tickets ranked: World Cup Final, $30,000+ tickets, Argentina and more
news.lede: These are the whoppers on the World Cup ticket market right now, going as high as $30,000. The FIFA World Cup has officially returned to North America for the first time since USA '94. Thirty years ago, despite soccer's lower profile in the region, that tournament shattered attendance metrics to become one of the most commercially successful iterations in history. Now, the newly expanded 48-team tournament is deep into its high-stakes knockout stages.

news.title: World Cup 2026: England to wear all-white kit and Argentina navy blue in semi-finals
news.lede: England's Jude Bellingham has scored six goals at the 2026 World Cup, while Argentina's Lionel Messi has scored eight. England will wear their all-white home kit to face Argentina in the semi-finals of the World Cup in Atlanta on Wednesday. Holders Argentina will be in their navy blue away strip, with black shorts and socks, for the game at the Atlanta Stadium (20:00 BST), which will be shown live on the BBC. They have only worn the darker kit once during the tournament, in their 3-1 win against Jordan in the group stage.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time and will have an eye on the gold medals at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' group stage and knockout stage fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 4-2 vs Croatia (Dallas) Tuesday 23 June: D 0-0 vs Ghana (Boston) Saturday 27 June: W 2-0 vs Panama (New York) Wednesday 1 July (R32): W 2-1 vs DR Congo (Atlanta) Monday 6 July (R16): W 3-2 vs Mexico (Mexico City) Saturday 11 July (QF): W 2-1 aet vs Norway (Miami) Wednesday 15 July (SF): vs Argentina (Atlanta, 21:00).

news.title: World Cup 2026 TV schedule: How to watch every knockout game live in the UK
news.lede: The last-32 matches are all free-to-air on the BBC and ITV. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: The World Cup’s final four are FIFA’s four top-ranked teams. Is this a coincidence? Not exactly
news.lede: FIFA implemented a new system for the World Cup draw and it has worked out as they might have hoped.
